behaviors.description: Rows with missing required fields are rejected and appear in the dead-letter queue with the original row number.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is validator -> DLQ handoff plus row-number propagation (MOCK-MISS yes for cross-module state handoff). REAL-INFRA is not required by the behavior text; an in-process queue/DB fake can reproduce this. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real S3/network/OS events/subprocess, only correct module wiring and payload contents.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline components together in-process with a fixture CSV containing missing required fields; assert invalid rows are emitted to the DLQ sink with the exact original CSV row numbers and valid rows are not emitted.

behaviors.description: The transformation correctly converts date strings in multiple formats (ISO 8601, US, European) to a single canonical format.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure transformation logic on date strings, with no required module handoff or I/O boundary (MOCK-MISS no). REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP are all no. Rejected Integration because behavior text is explicitly about conversion correctness, not inter-module coordination.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven unit tests over representative ISO 8601, US, and European date inputs (including ambiguous cases) asserting exact canonical output or explicit validation failure.

behaviors.description: A CSV file with 500,000 rows completes processing within the configured timeout without running out of memory.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real runtime resource behavior (timeout + memory pressure) under large load (REAL-INFRA yes: OS scheduling/memory/process limits). In-process mocks can hide OOM/GC/backpressure effects (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly requires end-to-end performance and memory characteristics at scale.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real pipeline process against a 500k-row CSV in an environment matching production memory/time limits; assert completion before configured timeout and peak memory below limit, with no OOM or process kill.

behaviors.description: When the S3 bucket returns a transient 503 error, the pipeline retries with exponential backoff rather than failing the entire batch.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text anchors a real infra condition (S3 transient 503) plus timing behavior (exponential backoff). Real HTTP/S3 SDK interaction can fail differently than stubs (REAL-INFRA yes, MOCK-HIDE yes for retryability/classification/timing semantics). Rejected Integration because behavior is about handling actual upstream transient failures, not only local retry-loop logic.
behaviors.test_description: Run pipeline against a test S3 endpoint/bucket path where initial GET/list calls return HTTP 503 and later succeed; verify retry count and backoff intervals increase exponentially and batch ultimately continues instead of terminal failure.

behaviors.description: Rows successfully written to PostgreSQL are not duplicated if the pipeline is restarted mid-batch.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is durability/idempotency across process restart and database state persistence (REAL-INFRA yes: subprocess restart + real DB transaction/constraint semantics). In-process fakes often miss restart recovery and commit visibility edge cases (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration because behavior text explicitly includes restart mid-batch, which crosses process/OS boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Start a real pipeline run writing to PostgreSQL, force termination mid-batch, restart pipeline with same input/checkpoint state, then assert exactly-once outcome (no duplicate target rows) using primary key or idempotency key verification.
